channel.title: INtopBackup
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INtopBackup, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: U.S.-Israeli strikes continue across Iran; Iranian drones hit Azerbaijan
item.description: The U.S. and Israel say they conducted new strikes inside Iran overnight, targeting ballistic missile launchers. Iran claims it struck a U.S. oil tank...

item.title: Wall Street is betting on tariff refunds after Supreme Court ruling
item.description: When the Supreme Court struck down many of President Trump's tariffs, it left importers wondering how long they'd have to wait to get their money back...

item.title: In lawsuit, Minnesota accuses Trump administration of 'weaponizing' Medicaid funding
item.description: The federal government said the state should do more to fight fraud and is holding back funds. Minnesota officials say the attack is unfair as the sta...

item.title: March (Audio) Madness! Here are the finalists in NPR's College Podcast Challenge
item.description: From 75 campuses across 35 states, we've listened to hundreds of student entries to select the very best for NPR's College Podcast Challenge.

item.title: House to vote on Trump's war powers. And, Minnesota sues over halted Medicaid funding
item.description: The House is set to vote today on whether to constrain President Trump's authority to continue to wage war on Iran. And, Minnesota sues the Trump admi...

item.title: Nepalis vote in first election since Gen Z uprising for change
item.description: This election will test whether the country is ready for a new, untested generation to shape its future.

item.title: Ethiopia experiments with 'smart' police stations that have no officers
item.description: A project to introduce unmanned sites is part of a broader adoption of digital technologies.

item.title: How Kurdish forces could help the U.S. fight the Iran war
item.description: Kurdish forces surrounding Iran may jump into the fight against the Islamic Republic. CBS News' Courtney Kealy reports.

item.title: Britney Spears arrested in Southern California, Ventura County Sheriff's Office records show
item.description: According to Ventura County inmate records, Spears was arrested by the California Highway Patrol at 9:28 p.m. Wednesday and released at 6:07 a.m. on T...

item.title: Texas lawmaker admits 'lapse in judgement' in affair with aide
item.description: The admission from the married Republican, Tony Gonzales, comes after a House panel launched an ethics investigation.

item.title: Massachusetts governor pushes back after RFK Jr. calls out popular coffee chains
item.description: HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is challenging Starbucks and Dunkin' over the safety of high sugar drinks for teenagers as part of his Make Americ...

item.title: Companies are entitled to Trump tariff refunds, trade court rules
item.description: A federal court in New York ruled Wednesday that businesses that paid emergency tariffs invalidated by the Supreme Court are eligible for refunds.

item.title: Families turn to states for civil rights support as Trump dismantles the Education Department
DR Congo government says 200 killed after landslide at rebel-held mine
item.description: The M23, which controls the coltan-rich area, has not commented and the death toll is hard to verify.

item.title: Iran's drone and missile strikes continue, hit new ground as war spreads
item.description: As Iran and the U.S. vow to intensify their attacks, Gulf states are caught in the middle, and they're running low on interceptors to block Iran's ret...

item.title: Timelapse shows change in the flow of ships in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A timelapse of marine traffic shows the flow of ships has decreased in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: What to know about the investigation into Catholic priests in Rhode Island and sexual abuse charges
Last 2 names of 6 US soldiers who died in Kuwait attack identified by the Pentagon
item.description: The last two names of the six U.S. soldiers killed in a Kuwait attack have been released by the Pentagon, and they are from California and Iowa.
